title: BOM (Browser Object Model)
content:
### The Browser Object Model (BOM)

The **BOM** represents the browser window and provides objects to interact with the browser.

---

### The `window` Object

The **global object** in browser JavaScript. All global variables and functions are properties of `window`.

```js
console.log(window.innerWidth);    // Viewport width
console.log(window.innerHeight);   // Viewport height
console.log(window.scrollX);       // Horizontal scroll position
console.log(window.scrollY);       // Vertical scroll position

window.open("https://example.com");  // Open new tab
window.close();                       // Close window
window.scrollTo(0, 0);               // Scroll to top
```

---

### The `location` Object

Information about the **current URL** and methods to navigate:

```js
console.log(location.href);       // Full URL
console.log(location.protocol);   // "https:"
console.log(location.hostname);   // "example.com"
console.log(location.pathname);   // "/page"
console.log(location.search);     // "?id=5"
console.log(location.hash);       // "#section"

location.href = "https://google.com";  // Navigate to URL
location.reload();                      // Reload page
```

---

### The `navigator` Object

Information about the **browser** itself:

```js
console.log(navigator.userAgent);     // Browser info string
console.log(navigator.platform);      // OS platform
console.log(navigator.language);      // Browser language
console.log(navigator.onLine);        // Is user online?
console.log(navigator.geolocation);   // Geolocation API
```

---

### The `screen` Object

Information about the **user's screen**:

```js
console.log(screen.width);       // Screen width
console.log(screen.height);      // Screen height
console.log(screen.availWidth);  // Available width
console.log(screen.colorDepth);  // Color depth (bits)
```

---

### Dialog Boxes

```js
alert("Hello!");                        // Simple alert
const name = prompt("Enter name:");      // Input prompt
const confirm = confirm("Are you sure?"); // Yes/No dialog
```

> **Tip:** Dialog boxes block the page. Use them sparingly for better user experience.

quiz.question: Which object represents the browser window?
